title: Trump announces ceasefire with Iran and offers two-week window for diplomacy
subhead: The US president, who had vowed to annihilate Iran, announced a ceasefire with Tehran on Tuesday evening, which should lead to the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. Israel said it supported the agreement but insisted that its operations in Lebanon were unaffected.

title: French hostages return home after more than three years in Iranian prison
subhead: Cécile Kohler and Jacques Paris landed in Paris on Wednesday morning, following years of detention on espionage charges.

title: In Hungary, Vance backs Orban in last-ditch attempt five days before elections
subhead: The American vice president praised the Hungarian prime minister during a rally in Budapest on Tuesday. In power since 2010, the nationalist leader is not the favorite in the April 12 legislative elections.

title: In catacombs, museums, and labs, the delicate heritage of human remains
subhead: The Paris Catacombs reopen to the public on Wednesday, April 8, after five months of renovations. The timing offers a chance to reflect on how human remains are handled in cultural and research institutions. These singular relics pose ethical, technical and legal questions.

title: 'The US administration's dysfunctions and the Iranian regime's resilience are driving Trump further into an obsession with force'
subhead: Five weeks into the US bombing campaign against Iran, Donald Trump's trademark unpredictability has been overtaken by the unpredictable dynamics of the war itself, observes Le Monde columnist Gilles Paris.

Why is Donald Trump so mean?
subhead: By shamelessly flouting democratic norms and flaunting his amorality, brutality and bad faith, the American president restores pride to his humiliated electoral base, unsettles his opponents, and subverts the rules of the political game.

title: Are Trump's ideologues intellectuals or imposters?
subhead: Curtis Yarvin has become a leading figure in the Neo-Reactionary movement (also known as the Dark Enlightenment), which has now made it to the White House. But can this blogger really be considered an intellectual? Political theorist Arnaud Miranda and sociologist Olivier Alexandre debate the origins and roles of one of the thinkers who has Trump's ear.

title: On immigration, Spain moves against the tide of its European neighbors
subhead: Citing economic and moral arguments, Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez's Socialist government is preparing to grant legal status to 500,000 undocumented workers, most from Latin America. The plan brings hope to the 840,000 foreign nationals living without papers in Spain.
